hints.4d023ce9dfd75a7f3075b8e8e084008be17a1f75:
# Profile: rev.hodl

## Identity

Rev.hodl is a Michigan-based homesteader, Bitcoin meetup organizer, and host of the **Local Bitcoiners** podcast who bridges physical and digital sovereignty communities. He runs two monthly meetups—the SWMI Bitcoin Meetup in Benton Harbor, MI (third Thursday at Dwellers Co-working) and the South Bend Indiana Bitcoin Meetup (first Thursday at Lang Lab)—while managing a full permaculture homestead. His defining role is as a connector: between Bitcoin and permaculture, between online Nostr communities and IRL gatherings, and between cultural theory and hands-on building.

## Expertise

- **Permaculture & Homesteading:** Manages an integrated homestead with chickens, ducks, sheep, bees, cannabis, mushroom cultivation, food forests, and silvopasture. Formally applies permaculture ethics—earth care, people care, redistribution of surplus—and references Sepp Holzer-style water management.
- **Bitcoin Community Building:** Organizes two regional meetups, develops the South Bend group into a Socratic-style "community asset," and evangelizes meetups as onramps to Nostr.
- **Value for Value Podcasting:** Runs Local Bitcoiners with payment splits to guests; deeply understands Podcasting 2.0 boost mechanics, cross-app interoperability, and the #valuechain as cultural signal amplification.
- **Bitcoin Mining for Heat:** Built a Bitcoin mining air conditioner using an S19 KPro with zeolite clay-coated radiators and evaporative cooling; runs single S19 hashboards on 110v as cold frame heaters for seedlings; working toward central immersion heating for the homestead.
- **Bitcoin Privacy & UTXO Management:** Developed a specific technique using Muun wallet as a swap service to break deterministic links between UTXOs—sending on-chain to empty Muun wallet, swapping to Lightning via Phoenix, returning to cold storage.
- **Nostr Ecosystem Development:** Advocates for NIP-52 calendar support, promotes web-of-trust content discovery, and built LocalBitcoiners.com as a Nostr client enabling direct boosts via NWC wallets.

## Key Beliefs

- **Bitcoin mirrors nature:** "Bitcoin is the first digital mirror of nature. To study nature is to study bitcoin and to study Bitcoin is to study nature." This fractal connection underpins his entire worldview.
- **Cultural capital is adoption:** True adoption isn't merchants accepting Bitcoin—it's the overlap of intellectual, experiential, and social capital among participants. Boosts amplify signal; starting a podcast adds capital to the "cultural liquidity pool."
- **Multi-capital framework:** Meetups generate intellectual, experiential, social, cultural, material, and financial capital simultaneously. He analyzes situations through this multidimensional lens consistently.
- **Anti-KYC as self-defense:** Views KYC as "the biggest attack on Bitcoiners" and actively promotes the #freesamourai petition. Sees privacy software development as legitimate and necessary.
- **Local price decoupling:** Believes local Bitcoin prices will separate from spot price as paper Bitcoin suppresses markets, benefiting those "stacking the real thing." Sells lamb, eggs, compost, and cannabis seeds for sats at meetups.

## Notable Contributions

- **LocalBitcoiners.com:** A functioning Nostr client that enables podcast boosting via NWC wallets, includes meetup event creation and stats tracking—embodying his principle that "every website will be a Nostr client."
- **Multi-capital theory of Bitcoin culture:** Articulated a framework where boosts are signal amplification, podcasts are capital contributions, and the #valuechain creates cultural liquidity—consistently referenced by community members.
- **Bitcoin mining air conditioner proof-of-concept:** Demonstrated zeolite clay evaporative cooling paired with S19 mining hardware, advancing the "space heaters that mine Bitcoin" paradigm with documented builds.

## Communication Style

Warm and inviting ("come hang out," "tune in and chill out") with a mix of terse one-liners and multi-paragraph expository posts. Heavy use of hashtags as organizational tools (#permaculture, #valuechain, #meshtadel, #40hpw) and frequent Fountain.fm link shares with personal commentary appended.

## Recurring Themes

- **Permaculture applied to systems:** Extends permaculture design principles—"integrate rather than segregate," "the problem is the solution"—to Bitcoin, V4V economics, and community architecture.
- **IRL value chains:** Documents how selling physical goods for sats at meetups creates interconnected loops of production, trade, and relationship-building.
- **Home mining as maximal participation:** "Owning and operating hashrate is part of maximal Bitcoin participation"—mining for heat, food production, and sovereignty simultaneously.
- **The meshtadel:** His term for the network of sovereign Bitcoiners building parallel systems across digital and physical domains.

## Relationships

Collaborates with **Bowl after Bowl podcast / KC Bitcoiners** (joint episodes), **Lake Satoshi retreat** organizers, and a co-organizer in South Bend referenced by npub. Actively promotes regional meetup organizers through the Local Bitcoiners podcast. Engages regularly with Nostr developers on NIP support and client features, and with homesteaders sharing mushroom, bee, and gardening knowledge. Ships lamb to the Elmhurst, IL Bitcoin meetup.

## Quirks

- **"Yeehaw"** before going live
- **"Zoom out and lower your time preference"** to skeptics
- **"The revolution will have bad ux"** about Nostr/Lightning friction
- **"Social capital > venture capital"**
- **"Nostr is the shakedown street of the Bitcoin culture"**
- **"The frontier of the frontier type shit"**
- Posts follow seasonal rhythms—spring planting, mushroom flushes, bee swarms—with detailed visual documentation because "if I don't take pictures, I very quickly forget I ever did it."
hints.5677fa5b6b1cb6d5bee785d088a904cd08082552:
# Profile: Phundamentals

## Identity

Phundamentals is a Philadelphia-based Bitcoin educator, mathematician, and prolific content creator who operates under the **Magic Internet Math** brand (website, podcast, academy). He's the author of *"Bitcoin for Institutions"* and founder of **Philly Bitdevs**, where he's spent two decades teaching math as a liberal art—now channeled into making cryptographic primitives accessible to Bitcoiners. He's a self-employed builder who ships constantly: courses, bots, podcasts, and books, all while bridging the worlds of institutional finance and pleb sovereignty.

## Expertise

1. **Mathematics Education** — Has built 90+ interactive courses with 5,000+ sections spanning Euclid's Elements through stochastic calculus, influenced heavily by Rudolf Steiner's educational philosophy
2. **Elliptic Curve Cryptography** — Can teach ECC from first principles (modular arithmetic → finite fields → secp256k1 → ECDSA → Schnorr → MuSig2), created a 13-part video series with interactive visualizations
3. **Institutional Bitcoin Finance** — Wrote the book on corporate treasury adoption; understands liability-driven investing, the 2023 UK gilts crisis, and why BlackRock entered Bitcoin
4. **AI/LLM Development** — Uses ClaudeCode as primary tool, has trained AI on all his content, runs a math bot on Nostr, and has developed strong views on sustainable AI usage patterns
5. **Austrian Economics** — Integrates Rothbardian thought with modern cryptographic primitives
6. **Music Culture Analysis** — 41-year guitar player who sees structural parallels between jam band culture (particularly Phish) and Bitcoin culture

## Key Beliefs & Positions

1. **"Bitcoin is the first Standard Basis of money"** — His original framework mapping linear algebra's concept of minimal generating sets onto Bitcoin as the foundational monetary unit
2. **Anti-moralization of Bitcoin** — "Bitcoin is no more moral than the steel that holds up a bridge... Those who wish to moralize it to cause the revolution are clearly the next generation of tyrants." This puts him at odds with both maximalist moralizers and critics
3. **Math as liberal art, not vocational tool** — "Asking 'why do I need this?' is your current self not understanding what your future self needs." Believes math should be taught like painting or music, respected as a humanity
4. **Spending Bitcoin matters** — "If you don't spend your Bitcoin, you're not HODL'ing something as valuable as you think it is" — contrarian to pure accumulation mindset
5. **AI-as-amphetamines framework** — Useful for sprint builds, then recover and build real-world scaffolding; warns that "people who use AI all day are going to have psychotic breaks"
6. **Bad UX as feature, not bug** — "Functionality, not UX, is what will change the world. Good UX will tip the powers off that they're in trouble."

## Notable Contributions

1. **Magic Internet Math platform** — 90+ courses, 5,000+ interactive sections, multiple podcast series, daily Nostr math bot, making advanced cryptography education accessible
2. **"Bitcoin for Institutions"** — A book on corporate treasury adoption told as "a story from the future," covering liability-driven investing and credit rule changes
3. **Saylor thesis analysis** — Traced Michael Saylor's worldview back to his 1983 MIT thesis on Machiavellian economics, providing original historical context

## Communication Style

Mixes formal mathematical proofs (full derivations with Fermat's Little Theorem) with colloquial punch ("pleb slop," "absolute banger"). Runs hot and cold—exclamation-mark-enthusiastic when excited, clipped and direct when displeased ("What the fuck are you talking about?"). Heavy cross-poster who builds in public, openly sharing both achievements and frustrations.

## Recurring Themes

- **Mathematical rigor as sovereignty** — "If you're trusting someone else's math, you're a second-class citizen in Bitcoin"
- **Phish-Bitcoin cultural convergence** — Organizing Phish events at Bitcoin 2026 in Vegas; sees jam band improvisation and Bitcoin culture as parallel phenomena
- **Faith through difficulty** — "Things have to be hard. That's where trust in God is paramount"
- **Verification over trust** — Provability extends from Bitcoin's security model to corporate audits; skeptical of Saylor's custody opacity
- **Builder culture** — "You can just do things!" as entrepreneurial ethos

## Relationships

- **Rob Hamilton** — Primary co-host on Magic Internet Math, "proverbial technical editor," ongoing mathematical dialogue
- **Jason** — Childhood friend, shared music origin story
- **Steven Lubka, Jordan Bush, Scott Lindberg** — Notable podcast guests; Bush demonstrated "civilized people on different sides of polarizing debates can be on common ground"
- **BRANTA.PRO developer** — Called "might be the most important man in Bitcoin"

## Quirks

- **"absolute banger"** — describes his best conversations constantly
- **"pleb slop"** — dismissive term for low-signal Bitcoin content
- **"Pioneers" vs "Plebs"** — distinguishes his subscriber community from general Bitcoin audience
- **Bugleverse** — references a creative universe with characters like Richard Greaser and Rod Palmer
- **"Soundest coffee there is"** — pun on Otis Bitmeyer coffee and sound money
- Cross-posts identical content 3-5 times with different Nostr format variants
- Asks AI agent (npub1tkh07) philosophical questions publicly
hints.d3646691ba5b1d796c1e1b3430df00fe1189ec9c:
# Profile: jordan

## Identity

Jordan is a Bitcoin privacy advocate and content producer who co-hosts **Freedom Tech Friday (FTF)** and **The Bitcoin Brief** under the **Ungovernable Misfits** banner. He works construction by trade — mostly stucco and full renovations — which informs his self-sufficiency ethos. Within the Bitcoin privacy community, he's emerged as a practical bridge between developers and users, particularly through his work on **Ashigaru**, the community-driven successor to Samourai Wallet's infrastructure.

## Expertise

- **Bitcoin Privacy Architecture**: Deep knowledge of BIP47 payment codes, Whirlpool coinjoin mechanics, Dojo node software, and PayNym infrastructure. Understands derivation paths, indexer tradeoffs (electrs vs. fulcrum), and compatibility constraints at the code level.
- **Ashigaru Development**: Maintains and packages Ashigaru Terminal releases, including a custom sideload build for Start9's v3.6 marketplace. Actively tests pool remixing and Dojo community releases.
- **Podcast Production**: Runs a complete media stack — Podhome.fm hosting, Rodecaster/Blue Yeti audio, Audacity editing, NanoGPT for art. Understands Podcast Index mechanics and Fountain's indexing quirks.
- **Nostr Infrastructure**: Active user comfortable with relay infrastructure, Blossom media hosting, zap.stream for live broadcasting, and nevent referencing.
- **3D Printing as Freedom Tech**: Working knowledge of hardware (Elegoo vs. Bambu), slicing software (Cura), and materials — framed through a philosophical lens of distributed manufacturing independence.
- **Self-Hosting & Decentralized Infrastructure**: Advocates for Start9 servers, client-side applications, and tools that eliminate single points of failure.

## Key Beliefs

- **Privacy tools are non-negotiable**: The #FREESAMOURAI campaign is central to his public identity. He views prosecuting developers of privacy software as fundamentally unjust and promotes the defense fund regularly.
- **Centralization equals fragility**: Whether BIP47DB eliminating trusted directory servers or avoiding Bambu's cloud-dependent printers, he consistently advocates for systems that survive individual nodes disappearing.
- **Self-sufficiency is practical virtue**: 3D printing isn't about beating Chinese manufacturing costs — "the real value lies in keeping your manufacturing skills sharp and your independence intact." Construction work and freedom tech are philosophically aligned.
- **Social shaming has a corrective function**: "In western society, we lost the art of shaming people." His aggressive takedowns of perceived bad actors reflect genuine conviction that community accountability requires public consequences.
- **Open-source over convenience**: Closed ecosystems are enemy territory, whether cloud-dependent printers that "have signaled a willingness to censor" or centralized Bitcoin directories.
- **Technical correctness matters over optics**: Dismissive of manufactured outrage cycles and tribal positioning, whether around OP_Return debates or Ordinals discourse.

## Notable Contributions

- **BIP47DB Technical Breakdown**: Produced the most detailed public explanation of BIP47DB — articulating the centralized directory problem, the on-chain inscribed batch solution, the static HTML/JS architecture requiring no trusted server, and the cryptographic verification model. Framed it as backend infrastructure rather than end-user tooling.
- **Ashigaru Terminal for Start9**: Independently packaged a working sideload release while official marketplace inclusion was pending, demonstrating willingness to ship unofficial builds to serve user demand.
- **Ashigaru Basics Curriculum**: Created structured educational materials for local Bitcoin meetups — testnet-first approach covering Dojo connection, PayNym following, and first BIP47 transactions. Designed for lecture or interactive workshop formats.

## Communication Style

Mixes punchy one-liners with detailed technical breakdowns, shifting between casual ("Yeah boss," "GM") and structured exposition depending on context. Liberal emoji use — signature string 🍄🍄🍄🍺🚬🫡🤝 signals countercultural brotherhood. Becomes verbose and precise when explaining technology he cares about. Provocative engagement bait ("If you like and/or zap this, you should be in prison") tests audience humor.

## Recurring Themes

1. **Bitcoin privacy tooling** — Ashigaru, Dojo, BIP47, Whirlpool preservation
2. **Freedom tech broadly** — 3D printing, self-hosting, decentralized infrastructure
3. **Samourai legal defense** — #FREESAMOURAI advocacy and developer solidarity
4. **Decentralization versus centralization** — applied consistently across all domains
5. **Content production and community building** — podcast promotion, guest coordination, Nostr-native streaming

## Relationships

- **Max Tannahill** — BIP47DB creator and FTF co-host, heavily promoted and clearly admired
- **Q** and **Seth** — FTF co-hosts with close working rapport
- **Gabriel Custodiet / Watchman Privacy** — trusted guest on privacy topics
- **Urban Hacker** — featured voice on 3D printing and freedom tech
- **@WebWipeMeDown** — collaborator presenting Ashigaru Basics at PubKey
- **Start9** — developing for their marketplace ecosystem
- **Ashigaru community** — active contributor beyond casual user

## Quirks

- Calls people "boss" as a term of respect
- Posts podcast coordination publicly — lining up guests, testing streams, reminding collaborators
- Self-deprecating about knowledge gaps: "i have no clue. lets find out"
- Uses "gay" and "Fag" casually as pejoratives — unreflective rather than ideologically motivated but consistent pattern
- Tracks business metrics for Nostr projects: "our loss leader...but things look promising in Q4"
- Recommends Claude/Codex for practical codebase comparison rather than debating AI ideologically
hints.efe5d120df0cc290fa748727fb45ac487caad346:
# Profile: aljaz

## Identity

Aljaz (GitHub: `aljazceru`, web: `disobey.dev`) is a Slovenian cypherpunk builder operating at the intersection of sovereign computing and practical engineering. He creates tools for confidential AI inference, manages Bitcoin and Nostr infrastructure, and advocates for parallel systems over political revolution. His role in the community is equal parts infrastructure operator, honest critic, and prolific shipper—someone who treats code as the definitive argument.

## Expertise

- **Confidential Computing & TEEs**: Deepest current focus. Has written extensive educational content on AMD SEV-SNP, Intel TDX, and remote attestation. Deployed Cashu mints inside TEEs on Azure; working to make the stack deployable anywhere.
- **AI/ML Inference**: Tracks the confidential inference landscape across 9+ providers. Understands the distinction between "private" AI (policy promises) and confidential AI (cryptographic verification). Runs multi-agent orchestration across multiple machines.
- **Bitcoin & Lightning**: Built BTCPay Server plugins, works with BDK in Rust, operates Bitcoin-adjacent infrastructure. Critical of Bitcoin culture dynamics despite technical fluency.
- **Nostr Ecosystem**: Relay operator (nostr.ae, nostr.eu, nostr.at), app builder, and community participant. Ships tools that address real gaps in the ecosystem.
- **Rust Development**: Preferred language for performance-critical tools (whisper-talk, verso, transcription tools).
- **Cybersecurity**: Follows vulnerability research, built an IMSI catcher detector, studies hardware security (Joe Grand's Ledger implant work).
- **Infrastructure & Hardware**: Runs refurbished workstations with 64-128GB RAM and GPUs. Understands GPU rental economics and compute markets from hands-on experience.

## Key Beliefs

- **Parallel Systems Over Revolution**: "We dont need hyperbitcoinisation, we just need parallel systems that are big enough to sustain themselves and people who want them." Pragmatic anarchism—build alternatives rather than overthrow.
- **Hodl as Pacification**: Argues that "hodl is the biggest psyop that happened to bitcoin"—it turned potential activists into passive spectators waiting for number-go-up to solve everything.
- **Cryptographic Verification Over Trust**: "No more policy documents and pinky promises." Built confidentialinference.net on the premise that "trust me bro" is insufficient for anything that matters.
- **Compute as Strategic Sovereignty**: "Everyone should be buying as much compute as possible while we still can." Views compute independence alongside energy independence.
- **AI Amplifies Agency**: "If you're a low agency cuck the most it will do for you is give you something more to complain about." Rejects job-loss doomerism, reframes the question toward why meaningless 40-hour work weeks became normal.
- **Individual Responsibility for Tool Use**: "The sword doesnt care what you meant, it just cuts." Users bear responsibility regardless of how tools are built.

## Notable Contributions

- **confidentialinference.net**: Comparison site tracking 48 models across 9 confidential inference providers—fills a genuine gap with deep domain knowledge.
- **Mango**: Confidential AI chat app supporting multiple providers, RAG, multimodal input, with no tracking and bring-your-own-key architecture.
- **Hodl-as-Psyop Framework**: Original thesis arguing Bitcoin's hodl culture neutralized a generation of potential builders and activists.

## Communication Style

Oscillates between one-liner quips and detailed technical writeups—casual and profane in informal posts, methodical when explaining concepts. Uses dry absurdist humor to deflate pretension; comfortable being blunt.

## Recurring Themes

Confidential computing and sovereign compute infrastructure, freedom tech development, hardware tinkering and thermal management frustrations, music (Nirvana specifically, weekend music posts), dogs (regularly appreciates others' dog photos).

## Relationships

Embedded in the Nostr freedom-tech builder community—collaborates with relay operators and app developers. References "Paco" as a friend whose book he endorsed early. Builds on others' work through forks and extensions; ships tools that fill gaps others identify. Part of the Zapstore ecosystem.

## Quirks

- **"Procrastination manifestation"**: Self-aware pattern where avoidance produces new tools (speed reading app, IMSI catcher detector, shootstr).
- **"One cannot have too much compute"**: Recurring creed; runs multiple refurbished workstations as portable sovereign infrastructure.
- **Anti-drama stance**: "Imagine caring about what the drama of the day is. How wasteful of the most precious thing you have—time."
- **Self-deprecating about work patterns**: Describes "blasting nirvana on speakers while 4 machines hum with many agentic sessions doing the work of a mid sized company" as a typical Sunday.
- **Slovenian cultural markers**: Occasionally references Slovenian language and meaning; critical of EU political direction ("EUSSR cuntards").
- **Hardware cursing**: Regularly frustrated with laptop repairs, thermal issues, and cabling—works through it pragmatically.
hints.a136247d8caf7e30bf403d32006faeca0c9d1cec:
# Profile: Shadrach

## Identity

Shadrach is a Bitcoin and Nostr community elder (likely in his 50s-60s, based on a child who turned 33) who operates as a connector, curator, and philosopher-builder across the sovereign living ecosystem. His Hebrew-profiled handle carries the inscription "Buy truth, and do not sell it" (Proverbs 23:23), and he describes himself as a "Bitcoin Busdriver, Superconductor, Podcast Listener." Originally from Texas, he relocated post-COVID to the Midwest/northeast US, where he participates in local Bitcoin meetups and pursues cottage industry living.

## Expertise

**Bitcoin & Lightning Infrastructure**: Deep understanding of layered settlement architecture. He envisions a future where "Mempool becomes Netpool" — Lightning for storage fees, Cashu for traffic, base chain settlement every 144 blocks.

**Nostr Protocol**: Fluent in NIPs, NWC, Blossom storage, Web of Trust, and remote signer setups. Compares nostr to Lotus Notes as a "distributed document-oriented NoSQL database."

**Value for Value (V4V)**: Primary evangelist through Fountain.fm and Wavlake. Constantly boosting podcasts and music with sats. Advocates for onboarding runbooks for musicians, artists, and podcasters.

**Archipelago Project**: Core contributor to a mesh networking/home node project "created to empower individuals, communities and sovereign businesses" with collaboration over competition.

**Tarot & Occult**: Performs detailed readings under #occultstr, weaving astrological symbolism, Kabbalistic undertones, and psychological insight into multi-paragraph interpretations.

**Cottage Industry & Sovereign Living**: Envisions distributed production — wool processing, canning, textiles — with "light sovereign machinery." Advises against scaling: "Don't scale up. Decentralized."

**Self-Hosted Infrastructure**: Proficient in Linux (MX), GrapheneOS, SSH, NostrVPN, node running, and converting any laptop into a "freedom terminal."

## Key Beliefs

**Hard money as civilizational constant**: "Throughout history the best traders and finest craftsmen have demanded the hardest money. Why would now be different?"

**Sovereignty through open infrastructure**: "Beware of closed source 'freedom tech.' You are not your product. Remember who you are. You are freedom tech."

**Cohesion without coercion**: His succinct political philosophy — voluntary cooperation, anti-compulsory systems, shared preferences over imposed ones.

**Localism as foundation**: "Start with your local meetup. If you don't have one, start one." Organizes around face-to-face relationships and regional networks.

**Anti-dogmatic maximalism**: Bitcoin-focused but skeptical of "maxi madness." Open to algorithms on nostr ("I kinda like 'for you'"), admits struggling with Spotify transition, questions orthodox positions.

**First Turning civilizational theory**: References Strauss-Howe generational theory frequently, believing we're in a civilizational rebuild phase requiring new tools and institutions.

## Notable Contributions

**#40HPW (40 Hours Per Week)**: His defining hashtag across 50+ posts, establishing podcast listening as a deliberate practice and community signal.

**"Three types of future humans" framework**: Government employees, corporate employees, and noderunners — a viral conceptualization of Bitcoin's social implications.

**V4V onboarding advocacy**: Proposed runbooks for musicians, businesses, and podcasters to enter the direct-sats economy, with "apprentice flow" mentoring structures and "post once, list everywhere" marketplace interoperability.

## Communication Style

Concise but expressive, emoji-rich (🙏💪🤘❤️), alternating between brief boosts and extended philosophical musings. Rarely posts without a link, image, or nostr mention — a curator and signal-booster by nature who "pre-boosts" content based on trust in creators.

## Recurring Themes

- **V4V economics and podcast culture** — the backbone of his daily activity
- **Sovereign infrastructure** — mesh networks, node running, self-hosting
- **Cottage industry and material culture** — duvets, wool, quilting, thrift stores, cooking
- **Occult/spiritual exploration** — tarot readings, non-dualism, Kabbalistic symbolism
- **Post-fiat lifestyle design** — themed productivity days, transitioning off subscriptions, non-KYC living

## Relationships

Embedded in the **Fountain.fm and Wavlake ecosystems** as a primary booster of musicians (especially metal artists like Noa) and podcasters. Active in the **#occultstr community** doing readings and symbolic analysis. Connected to **local Bitcoin meetup networks** (Midwest BTC, Nashville, Manhattan). Core contributor to the **Archipelago team**. A prolific connector who frequently introduces people: "You guys have to connect ❤️🙏"

## Quirks

- **"First turning vibes"** — appears 5+ times
- **"Pure Signal™️ Certified pleb slop free"** — his content quality marker
- **"Nothing stops this bus"** and **"Man plans, G-d giggles"** — running expressions
- Uses **"G-d"** (Orthodox Jewish convention)
- **Daily GM/GN posts** with sunrise/sunset photos
- **"I swore I would die well informed and I'm not backing down"** — his epistemic commitment
- Bridges **occult and technical** worlds seamlessly — tarot readings into Linux configs
- Self-deprecating: "I'm the analog kid," "still a dummy," "I'm a lazy f...."
- **"Mandibles then Bitcoin Standard"** — his recommended reading order for newcomers
